name: Lobio Tkemali (Red Beans W/Tamarind and Balsa
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Red Beans; Small, Soaked Overnight in Water
2 teaspoons Tamarind Concentrate (or 2 teaspoons Unsweetened Plum Butter)
1 teaspoon Chinese Chili and Garlic Paste
1/4 cup Cilantro Leaves; Finely Chopped (plus some for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
If unable to find Tamarind Concentrate, use 2 teaspoons Unsweetened Plum Butter plus 2 tablespoons Fresh Lemon Juice as a substitute.
Combine beans with onion, carrot, and celery in a soup pot. Add enough water to cover beans by 3 inches and bring to boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until tender but not mushy (about 55 minutes or more).
Simmer prunes and balsamic vinegar in a saucepan for about 15 minutes. Remove prunes with slotted spoon; reserve vinegar. Finely chop prunes.
Add tamarind concentrate to reserved vinegar; let stand until dissolved (about 10 minutes). Stir well.
Drain beans, discard onion, carrot, and celery. Place beans in serving dish and allow to cool.
Whisk together diluted tamarind mixture, chili paste, and olive oil in a bowl. Add chopped prunes, coriander seeds, fenugreek; blend well. Toss beans with tamarind mixture.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Stir in cilantro leaves. Refrigerate, covered for at least 2 hours before serving. Garnish with additional cilantro and onion rings.
